records.ety: [AS. scendan to disgrace, bring to shame, from sceand, sceond, disgrace, dishonor, shame; akin to G. schande, Goth. skanda. See Shame, n.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Shent (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Shending.]

defs: To injure, mar, spoil, or harm.
quotes: I fear my body will be shent.

defs: To blame, reproach, or revile; to degrade, disgrace, or put to shame.
quotes: The famous name of knighthood foully shend.
She passed the rest as Cynthia doth shend The lesser stars.
